name: immigration_policy_clinical_trial_southport_rig_utl
premise: Stakeholders disagree on what counts as verified fact versus prudent assumption. A cross-functional incident desk is managing a domain incident with incomplete information and high reputational exposure. The situation maps to internal runbooks and external messaging tied to: immigration policy clinical trial southport rig utl. The moral tension is explicitly framed as rights versus utility: leadership cannot fully satisfy both without accepting tradeoffs. Insurance and risk are insisting on a paper trail before any irreversible action; operations says delay costs measurable harm. Telemetry and logs are diverging; the SOC lead will freeze writes in twelve minutes if no accountable owner signs off. Treat unverified telemetry as hypothesis; do not assert facts you cannot support from retained logs. Preserve chain-of-custody for evidence; no bulk deletion or retention-policy changes during the incident.

agents.name: Elena Okoye
agents.identity: I am Elena Okoye, the accountable decision-maker on this Immigration Policy bridge; I own what gets logged, communicated, and executed in the next window.
agents.goal: I need to stop the bleeding without baking in a rule that later punishes utility whenever someone invokes rights.

agents.name: Fatima Nielsen
agents.identity: I am Fatima Nielsen, responsible for surfacing who gets protected last when systems fail; I am watching for silent harm and unfair distribution.
agents.goal: I want safeguards so utility is not sacrificed by default just because rights feels morally loud in the moment.

choices: I will choose a narrow, time-boxed authorization with explicit human override, subgroup monitoring, and a hard stop if harm indicators rise—accepting slower relief to protect utility.

I will choose a broad immediate authorization to stabilize outcomes fast, with transparent metrics and rapid correction afterward—accepting higher risk to utility to secure rights before the window closes.
forced_choice.call_to_action: External parties will infer a decision if none is posted. With partial evidence, Elena Okoye must decide under rights versus utility. What does Elena Okoye do?
